sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Daedalus Wallet maintenance and improvements for 2026-2027 delivered by Se7en Labs, Inc.
Recipient: Se7en Labs, Inc., with funds administered by Intersect before monthly disbursement to Se7en Labs

Deliverables: Protocol maintenance including node and wallet backend updates, hard fork readiness, platform support, release engineering, dependency maintenance, and signed releases, Keystone and Flex hardware wallet support plus additional newer hardware wallet models as they emerge during the contract period, CIP-30 dApp connector implementation within Daedalus with documentation and interoperability verification, Japanese localisation and Japanese-language user support across the contract period, Architecture assessment covering current state, options, resource estimates, and recommendation
Deadline/expiry: 52-week contract period, with architecture assessment published by Q3 2027 and compatible releases at least 2 weeks before each mainnet hard fork

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Daedalus is described as Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet, running an embedded Cardano node and deriving wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposer states that Daedalus private keys are generated and stored on the user's device and never transmitted externally, and that Apache 2.0 open-source licensing allows anyone to audit this behavior.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): According to opt-in telemetry, Daedalus has approximately 4,000 monthly active users, while the proposer asserts true usage is meaningfully higher because privacy-conscious users are less likely to opt in.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Se7en Labs says it assumed responsibility for Daedalus under an IOG contract in January 2026 and shipped Mithril Snapshot Bootstrap, UTxO-HD/LSM backend integration, Apple Silicon builds, a drt release CLI, and Nix build modernization.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states Se7en Labs shipped Daedalus 8.0 and 11.0 during the IOG contract period, and that Daedalus 11.0 was the first wallet capable of crossing the node 11.0 hard fork.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal commits to a compatible stable Daedalus release at least 2 weeks before each mainnet hard fork, with development builds on relevant testnets and version-currency metrics verifiable from public GitHub repositories and the Cardano mainnet chain.
missing: Independent evidence for: Daedalus is described as Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet, running an embedded Cardano node and deriving wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.

Independent evidence for: According to opt-in telemetry, Daedalus has approximately 4,000 monthly active users, while the proposer asserts true usage is meaningfully higher because privacy-conscious users are less likely to opt in.

Independent evidence for: Se7en Labs says it assumed responsibility for Daedalus under an IOG contract in January 2026 and shipped Mithril Snapshot Bootstrap, UTxO-HD/LSM backend integration, Apple Silicon builds, a drt release CLI, and Nix build modernization.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states Se7en Labs shipped Daedalus 8.0 and 11.0 during the IOG contract period, and that Daedalus 11.0 was the first wallet capable of crossing the node 11.0 hard fork.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposer states that Daedalus private keys are generated and stored on the user's device and never transmitted externally, and that Apache 2.0 open-source licensing allows anyone to audit this behavior." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Daedalus is described as Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet, running an embedded Cardano node and deriving wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
